verified.notes: The provided search results do not contain specific eligibility details for Health First Colorado Buy-In (QMB, SLMB, QI) programs. The results discuss general Health First Colorado changes for 2026-2027 (work requirements, immigration restrictions, retroactive coverage changes) but do not address Buy-In program age thresholds, asset limits, or application phone numbers. To obtain authoritative data on QMB/SLMB/QI eligibility for seniors, contact Health First Colorado directly or consult official Colorado Department of Health Care Policy & Financing documentation.

verified.notes: SNAP has no age requirement for seniors or any population; eligibility is based on income (200% FPL) and household composition only. No asset test found in search results for SNAP. Income limits effective October 1, 2025 through September 30, 2026. Phone number is Hunger Free Colorado's SNAP hotline; official state CDHS application portal is Colorado PEAK or MyCoBenefits app. Search results do not contain asset limit information for SNAP in Colorado.

verified.notes: Age requirement (55+) confirmed from U.S. Department of Labor official source. Income limit is 125% of federal poverty level (not a fixed dollar amount; varies annually). Official phone number for Colorado SCSEP not provided in search results; Colorado DHHS referral URL available at https://cdhs.colorado.gov/benefits-assistance/employment-assistance/senior-community-service-employment-program. Program is active as of 2026 per NCOA source noting recent federal funding transitions.
